name: Shenlong
primary_sources: Karl Shuker: Dragons. A Natural History. Simon & Schuster, New York 1995, ISBN 0-684-81443-9, p. 89

visual_attributes.size_and_scale: large, spanning several meters in length
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: long, sinuous body with a flowing mane and whiskers

domain_specific_visual.weather_control: surrounded by storm clouds and rain

functional_attributes.primary_function: dragon god of the tempest and master of rain

domain_specific_functional.weather_punishment: causes droughts or floods if angered

relationships.description: Both are significant dragons in Chinese mythology.

contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: symbol of imperial power and agricultural prosperity
